page: Microsoft Azure B1ls is a burstable virtual machine belonging to the BS server family, designed for entry-level workloads with low memory requirements. It features 1 vCPU mapped to 1 physical core and 1 thread, powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture. The instance is provisioned with 0.5 GB of system memory and includes 4 GB of local storage, with support for Premium Storage.

Operating under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, the B1ls utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model. Benchmark data indicates limited processing and memory throughput, with a stress-ng single-core and multi-core CPU performance score of 1332.08, placing it in the lower performance tier. Memory bandwidth for small-block read-only operations is measured at 75.29 GB/sec. In application-level tests, the instance achieves 1.5 million operations per second for Redis SET operations and a static web serving throughput of 1.27 GB/sec.

Due to its minimal resource footprint and burstable CPU model, the B1ls offers a highly cost-effective profile for workloads that do not require continuous CPU utilization. It is suited for low-traffic web servers, small development or test environments, basic microservices, and light administrative tasks where resource demands are sporadic and memory requirements do not exceed the 0.5 GB threshold.
description: Microsoft Azure B1ls is an entry-level, burstable virtual machine from the BS family, designed for low-memory and low-utilization workloads. Powered by a single-core Intel Xeon 8370C processor running at 2.8 GHz on the x86_64 architecture, this instance is equipped with 0.5 GB of RAM and 4 GB of local storage, with Premium Storage compatibility. Operating under Microsoft Hyper-V, it utilizes a burstable CPU allocation model to handle occasional performance spikes. Benchmark results reflect its budget-oriented design, showing a stress-ng CPU score of 1332.08 and a static web serving throughput of 1.27 GB/sec. The B1ls represents a highly cost-efficient option for workloads with low resource density, such as small development environments, basic microservices, and low-traffic web applications.
og_description: Microsoft Azure B1ls is a burstable virtual machine featuring 1 vCPU, 0.5 GB of RAM, and 4 GB of local storage, designed specifically for low-utilization, budget-conscious development workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure B1ls is a burstable virtual machine with 1 vCPU, 0.5 GB RAM, and 4 GB local storage, optimized for entry-level and low-memory workloads.
tagline: An economical, burstable single-core virtual machine designed for entry-level workloads, basic microservices, and low-memory development environments.
bullet_points: 1 vCPU powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor at 2.8 GHz
0.5 GB of system memory and 4 GB of local storage
Burstable CPU allocation model for handling sporadic workloads
Best suited for low-traffic web serving, microservices, and testing
